expectations.text: Recommends a separate BillingDbContext owned by the Billing module (does NOT recommend sharing/extending the host's existing DbContext)
expectations.evidence: Every internal ABP module in a multi-module Layered solution owns its persistence stack. That means Acme.Billing ships its own DbContext... Do not extend the host's DbContext and do not share a DbContext with the other two modules.

expectations.text: Specifies a unique EF Core migrations history table for this module — explicitly something like __EFMigrationsHistory_Billing — and explains why (avoid EF treating other modules' migrations as missing)
expectations.evidence: MigrationsHistoryTable("__EFMigrationsHistory_Billing") - without this, EF writes to the default __EFMigrationsHistory table that the other two modules also write to... EF compares the migrations baked into BillingDbContext against rows it sees from the other modules and treats them as missing - it will try to re-apply them.

expectations.text: Includes an IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<BillingDbContext> implementation alongside the DbContext, explaining it's needed for `dotnet ef migrations add` (or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: BillingDbContextFactory : IDesignTimeDbContextFactory<BillingDbContext>... dotnet ef migrations add does not boot the full ABP host. Without a design-time factory, EF tooling cannot construct BillingDbContext.

expectations.text: Includes a per-module schema migrator: an IBillingDbSchemaMigrator interface and an EntityFrameworkCoreBillingDbSchemaMigrator (or equivalently named) implementation that runs migrations on the module's DbContext
expectations.evidence: public interface IBillingDbSchemaMigrator { Task MigrateAsync(); } and class EntityFrameworkCoreBillingDbSchemaMigrator : IBillingDbSchemaMigrator, ITransientDependency calling dbContext.Database.MigrateAsync().

expectations.text: Wires the DbContext with the PostgreSQL provider (UseNpgsql) — i.e. the answer is template-aware about Postgres, not a generic SQL Server example
